thesis: Ship a working authentication and dashboard foundation that validates the core user journey before expanding into configuration and collaboration features.
narrative:
The first phase of Garura's development focuses on proving the core loop: a developer can authenticate, orient via the dashboard, and begin productive work within 30 seconds of session start. This is not about feature completeness — it is about validating that the intent-driven workflow produces a coherent, usable system end-to-end.

Once the foundation is validated through passing scenarios and real user sessions, the roadmap expands into configurability (Settings Management) and eventually team-scale features. Each horizon is gated on the previous one passing its scenario suite — no horizon begins until its predecessor is verified. This sequencing ensures that every capability we ship stands on a tested foundation rather than an assumed one.
assumptions: A single-user MVP is sufficient to validate the core workflow before investing in multi-tenancy
Email/password authentication covers 90% of target users — OAuth can be added post-MVP without architectural changes
Dashboard widget configuration stored as JSON is a valid approach at MVP scale — no query requirements across users
exclusions: Team and organization features are excluded from all three horizons — requires separate multi-tenancy architectural investment
Mobile application is excluded — web-first approach validates the product before committing to a second platform

epics.name: Authenticated Foundation
epics.description: Deliver a fully authenticated user experience with registration, login, password recovery, and a live dashboard as the primary post-login landing page

affected_workflows: Establishes the authenticated session that all future features build on
blast_radius.notes: This epic is the foundation — all subsequent epics depend on the auth and session infrastructure delivered here

intent.p1: Users must be able to create accounts and reach a live dashboard in under 2 minutes from first visit
intent.p2: The authentication system must be secure by default — no plaintext passwords, no token leakage, no account enumeration
intent.p3: Dashboard must provide immediate orientation value — a new user should understand what the system tracks within 30 seconds of viewing it

in_scope: Dashboard with metrics and activity widgets
must_not_break: CI pipeline must remain green throughout

success_scenarios.given: A new user discovers the product
success_scenarios.when: They complete registration and first login
success_scenarios.then: They reach a populated dashboard within 2 minutes of arriving at the landing page

success_scenarios.given: An existing user returns after a week
success_scenarios.then: Their dashboard layout is exactly as they left it

failure_conditions: Auth endpoints are accessible without HTTPS in production — immediate security halt
Dashboard loads take more than 5 seconds on first paint — performance gate blocks release

epics.name: User Configurability
epics.description: Extend the authenticated experience with user-controlled preferences including profile editing, notification configuration, and account management

existing_systems: Reads and writes to User and UserSettings tables established in E1
affected_workflows: Notification preferences affect email delivery behavior established in E1's password reset flow
integration_points: SendGrid notification opt-out must be respected by password reset flow
blast_radius.notes: E2 cannot begin until E1 scenario gate is fully passed — settings depend on the auth and user entity foundation

intent.p1: Users must be able to control their experience — profile, notifications, and account lifecycle — without contacting support
intent.p2: Settings changes must take effect immediately and persist reliably across sessions
intent.p3: Account deletion must be available to meet basic data privacy expectations

must_not_break: E1 authentication flow must remain unaffected by settings changes
Email change must require re-verification before taking effect

success_scenarios.then: The new name appears in the navigation header immediately without a page refresh

success_scenarios.when: A system event that would trigger a notification fires
success_scenarios.then: No email is dispatched to the user

failure_conditions: Email change takes effect without verification — creates account takeover vector
Notification opt-out is not respected by the password reset flow — violates user preference

feasibility.assessment: High confidence — well-understood JWT auth pattern with mature libraries; no novel technical risk
Medium confidence — WebSocket real-time updates add complexity; widget persistence as JSON is an untested pattern for this team

critical_blockers.blocker: SendGrid account and API key must be provisioned before F1 password reset can be tested end-to-end
critical_blockers.resolution: Provision SendGrid account in week 1 alongside initial project setup

open_questions.question: Should widget layout support drag-and-drop reordering in MVP or ship with a fixed layout first?
options: Fixed layout in MVP, drag-and-drop in E2
Drag-and-drop from day one using react-grid-layout

open_questions.question: What is the token expiry strategy — fixed 24-hour JWT or sliding expiry with refresh tokens?
options: Fixed 24-hour JWT (simpler, no refresh endpoint needed)
Sliding expiry with refresh tokens (more complex but better UX for active users)
